title: Historical Epidemiology
summary: Essays on epidemics as historical forces: ancient pathogens, colonial encounters, disease ecology, and the long memory of public health.
keywords: history historical epidemiology ancient colonial plague cocoliztli syphilis

title: Disease And War
summary: War, armies, migration, logistics, barracks, ships, and the infections that move through military systems.
keywords: war military revolutionary korean napoleon soldier oregon trail

title: Disease Ecology
summary: Disease stories where landscapes, reservoirs, vectors, cities, climate, and infrastructure explain more than the pathogen alone.
keywords: ecology vector mosquito climate environment raw milk zoonotic

title: Pathogen Geography
summary: Map-first work on how pathogens travel through ports, ships, animal reservoirs, water systems, roads, empires, and borders.
keywords: atlas geography pathogen maritime ship route port

title: Epidemiologic Methods
summary: Readable epidemiology methods, causal inference, risk communication, evidence grading, replication, and statistical thinking.
keywords: causal risk evidence baseline replication wearable meta-analysis studies

title: Wellness Claims
summary: Evidence-first writing on supplements, nutrition, wellness panics, influencer claims, and health stories that outrun the data.
keywords: wellness supplement raw milk gluten creatine melatonin detox sweetener dopamine

title: Neuroepidemiology
summary: Neuroepidemiology essays on migraine, cognition, epilepsy, dementia, Parkinson's disease, multiple sclerosis, and brain-health evidence.
keywords: migraine brain neuro alzheimer parkinson epilepsy seizure cognition ms

title: Pathogen Atlas
summary: An origin-and-spread atlas that links geography, uncertainty, papers, and prior writing instead of flattening pathogens into another story stream.
keywords: pathogen routes origin infectious disease spread

title: Maritime Disease Atlas
summary: A darker, map-forward atlas of shipboard disease ecology, ports, vectors, and the movement constraints of life at sea.
keywords: maritime ports ships yellow fever scurvy piracy vector

title: Revolutionary War Disease Atlas
summary: A historical epidemiology section focused on colonial and Revolutionary-era disease ecology, troop movement, and the public-health constraints around the founding period.
keywords: revolutionary war colonies smallpox inoculation military disease

title: Viking Health Atlas
summary: A Norse-world disease and health map that ties Greenland, Vinland, settlement ecology, and pre-Columbian contact arguments into one visual surface.
keywords: viking norse greenland vinland plague health

title: American Epidemic Timeline
summary: A carefully cited timeline of major U.S.-linked epidemics, outbreaks, and public-health turning points.
keywords: american epidemics timeline public health history outbreaks exhibit timeline

title: Pathogen Atlas
summary: A cited digital exhibit about pathogen origins, reservoirs, transmission ecology, historical spread, and evidentiary uncertainty.
keywords: pathogen reservoirs transmission origin uncertainty infectious disease spread atlas

title: Maritime Disease Atlas
summary: A cited map-first digital exhibit on ships, ports, quarantine, naval warfare, captivity, trade, migration, and maritime disease ecology.
keywords: maritime ports ships quarantine scurvy ship fever yellow fever migration exhibit atlas

title: Revolutionary War Disease Atlas
summary: A historical epidemiology section focused on colonial and Revolutionary-era disease ecology, troop movement, and the public-health constraints around the founding period.
keywords: revolutionary war colonies smallpox inoculation military disease atlas

title: Viking Health Atlas
summary: A Norse-world disease and health map that ties Greenland, Vinland, settlement ecology, and pre-Columbian contact arguments into one visual surface.
keywords: viking norse greenland vinland plague health atlas

title: Ebola virus disease
summary: Baseline snapshot created with 247 clustered item(s) across 158 source(s).
keywords: new_official_source suspected_case confirmed_case severity_or_death policy_or_travel new_geography healthcare_transmission laboratory_or_genomic medical_countermeasure_gap Africa Democratic Republic of the Congo

title: Hantavirus and cruise-ship outbreak
summary: Baseline snapshot created with 27 clustered item(s) across 18 source(s).
keywords: new_official_source severity_or_death policy_or_travel Global / Maritime Spain

title: Measles transmission and vaccination
summary: Baseline snapshot created with 3 clustered item(s) across 3 source(s).
keywords: new_official_source confirmed_case severity_or_death South Asia United States

title: Ebola virus disease
summary: Ebola remains a defining outbreak-desk disease because healthcare transmission, funeral practices, laboratory capacity, community trust, and international alarm can all move faster than the confirmed count.
keywords: Hemorrhagic fever High-fatality Outbreak containment ebola ebola virus disease ebolavirus orthoebolavirus bundibugyo bundibugyo virus bundibugyo ebolavirus bundibugyo virus disease viral hemorrhagic fever viral haemorrhagic fever

title: Hantavirus syndrome
summary: This is a rare but frightening severe-disease story where a single unusual cluster can force questions about travel safety, rodent exposure, and whether Andes-virus-style person-to-person spread is in play.
keywords: Zoonotic Hemorrhagic / cardiopulmonary Emerging hantavirus andes virus hantavirus pulmonary syndrome hps

title: Measles
summary: Measles is a clean reporter desk disease because it reveals vaccination gaps, school and household spread, travel-linked importation, and public-health capacity all at once.
keywords: Vaccine-preventable Respiratory High-R0 rubeola

title: Anthrax
summary: Anthrax is a strong local accountability story because livestock practices, slaughter exposure, and rural reporting gaps can hide serious outbreaks in plain sight.
keywords: Zoonotic Bacterial Occupational exposure bacillus anthracis cutaneous anthrax

title: Avian influenza A(H5N1)
summary: H5N1 is one of the few diseases where occupational exposure, food systems, animal surveillance, and pandemic-risk communications all converge in the same file.
keywords: Zoonotic Respiratory One Health h5n1 bird flu avian flu

title: Chikungunya
summary: Chikungunya is a high-quality reporter disease because explosive outbreaks can be large, visually obvious, and politically disruptive even when mortality stays low.
keywords: Vector-borne Arbovirus Resurgent chikungunya virus

title: Cholera
summary: Cholera is one of the clearest diseases for showing how water, war, displacement, climate shocks, and state capacity become mortality.
keywords: Waterborne Enteric Humanitarian settings acute watery diarrhea

title: Dengue
summary: Dengue is now one of the best climate-and-city disease stories on earth because mosquito range, urban crowding, and health-system stress all show up in it.
keywords: Vector-borne Arbovirus Urban transmission severe dengue

title: Diphtheria
summary: Diphtheria is a strong communications disease because it turns abstract vaccination decline into immediately legible airway and mortality risk.
keywords: Vaccine-preventable Respiratory Health-system stress signal corynebacterium diphtheriae

title: Hepatitis A
summary: Hepatitis A is useful because it can move between foodborne recall, encampment, homelessness, travel, and sanitation stories without changing pathogen.
keywords: Enteric Vaccine-preventable Foodborne hepatitis a virus hav

title: Legionnaires' disease
summary: Legionnaires' is a very good local-news and accountability disease because buildings, hospitals, hotels, and cooling towers turn infrastructure failure into human pneumonia clusters.
keywords: Environmental Water systems Facility outbreak risk legionella legionella pneumophila legionnaires

title: Malaria
summary: Malaria is a foundational global-health reporting file because it links endemic burden, climate, resistance, conflict, childhood mortality, and rural neglect.
keywords: Vector-borne Parasitic Endemic burden plasmodium falciparum plasmodium vivax

title: Marburg virus disease
summary: Marburg matters because even small clusters can force rapid reassessment of differential diagnosis, hospital preparedness, and regional spillover risk.
keywords: Hemorrhagic fever High-fatality Emerging marburg

title: Meningococcal disease
summary: Meningococcal disease is intensely newsworthy because it is fast, terrifying, often affects schools or young adults, and can force rapid public-health action.
keywords: Bacterial Respiratory Meningitis belt meningococcal meningococcal meningitis meningococcemia

title: Middle East respiratory syndrome (MERS)
summary: MERS remains a prototype hospital-cluster and travel-linked importation story where a modest case count can still matter a lot.
keywords: Respiratory Zoonotic Healthcare-associated mers mers-cov middle east respiratory syndrome

title: Mpox
summary: Mpox keeps generating news because social transmission networks, travel, stigma, clade differences, and global inequity collide in a single outbreak story.
keywords: Emerging Contact transmission Global surveillance monkeypox clade ib mpox clade iib mpox

title: Nipah virus disease
summary: Nipah is one of the few pathogens where even a small cluster can raise immediate questions about encephalitis, respiratory spread, hospital amplification, and pandemic risk.
keywords: Zoonotic High-fatality Emerging nipah

title: Norovirus
summary: Norovirus is highly useful for a desk because it produces the kind of explosive local outbreak that readers immediately understand, especially on cruise ships and in institutions.
keywords: Enteric Foodborne Cruise and congregate settings stomach bug viral gastroenteritis cruise ship norovirus

title: Oropouche virus disease
summary: Oropouche deserves attention because it can masquerade as dengue-like illness while signaling that arboviral ecology is shifting into new places.
keywords: Vector-borne Arbovirus Emerging Americas signal oropouche oropouche fever

title: Pertussis
summary: Pertussis repeatedly becomes news because it combines school spread, infant risk, vaccine debates, and underrecognized adult transmission.
keywords: Vaccine-preventable Respiratory Recurring resurgence whooping cough bordetella pertussis

title: Poliomyelitis / cVDPV2
summary: Polio is a near-eradication story where environmental detections, asymptomatic carriage, and vaccination politics matter as much as paralytic disease.
keywords: Vaccine-preventable Enteric Eradication target polio cvdpv2 vaccine-derived poliovirus

title: Rabies
summary: Rabies remains one of the starkest diseases in communications because nearly every symptomatic human case is a preventable systems failure.
keywords: Zoonotic High-fatality Neglected tropical dog rabies lyssavirus

title: Rift Valley fever
summary: Rift Valley fever is one of the best One Health reporting files because rainfall, livestock, mosquitoes, and human disease all move together.
keywords: Zoonotic Vector-borne Livestock-linked rvf rift valley fever virus

title: Yellow fever
summary: Yellow fever matters because a rise in sylvatic spillover, urban encroachment, or vaccine gaps can turn a seemingly remote arbovirus story into a major regional emergency.
keywords: Vector-borne Vaccine-preventable Sylvatic spillover yellow fever virus

title: Zika virus disease
summary: Zika matters because congenital outcomes, pregnancy guidance, travel messaging, and mosquito ecology can turn a mild-adult disease story into a major public-health communications event.
keywords: Vector-borne Congenital risk Emerging zika

title: Opportunities
summary: Work with Devin Teichrow on epidemiology, evidence, disease history, science communication, data projects, and public-health exhibits.
keywords: consulting collaboration epidemiology data science communication atlases public health
